summary: Integrate the AGPL cec-support-agent engine into the MIT MyOwn family (AllMyStuff app, MyOwnMesh substrate) over a PROCESS boundary, never a link. AllMyStuff drives the engine as a bundled Tauri sidecar binary (the exact mechanism it already uses for myownmesh-<triple>) and speaks a hand-mirrored, serde-only JSON contract (the exact discipline allmystuff-protocol/src/control.rs already uses to mirror the mesh control socket instead of importing myownmesh-core). No cec-* crate ever enters AllMyStuff's cargo graph, so the combined work stays MIT and the AGPL obligation is confined to the separately-distributed engine binary. The engine stays standalone: the only in-tree changes are additive, cold-start-preserving CLI seams (--inventory-keys, --json) plus one library trait (InventoryProvider) lifted out of the binary-private host_inventory(). Corpus-over-mesh and the inventory-to-config-keys adapter live in AGPL adapter crates that ship WITH the engine daemon (downward deps onto engine traits + myownmesh-core), never inside the app. De-identification is structural and pre-transport, so a row physically cannot carry identity before any transport touches it; the mesh adds per-peer roster + Role::Owner authorization on the wire. Two verified findings tighten the plan beyond the raw lenses: (1) HttpCorpus::query does NOT re-run admit() on received rows (store.rs:426-453) — the new MeshCorpus client MUST verify the ed25519 attestation on every row it receives on the QUERY path, closing a real read-path trust gap; (2) the constructor is SignOffAuthority::from_seed_hex, not from_seed (provenance/src/lib.rs:170), and the engine already reads CEC_SIGNOFF_SEED (main.rs:959-965), so identity unification needs no new engine API.
architecture:
Three rings, one direction, exactly two link clusters separated by a process boundary.

DEPENDENCY DIRECTION (a DAG, no cycle): app -> engine(process) -> mesh.
- AllMyStuff (MIT) cargo-depends on: myownmesh-core (MIT, git-tag-pinned) and allmystuff-* (MIT). It depends on the engine ONLY as a spawned child binary + a serde-only hand-mirrored JSON contract module. ZERO cec-* cargo edge.
- The engine workspace (AGPL) cargo-depends on: nothing upward. It never gains an allmystuff-* or myownmesh-* dependency. host_inventory enrichment is additive CLI input; the engine receives identity-free STRINGS, never an allmystuff type.
- The AGPL adapter crates (inventory-config-adapter, corpus-mesh-adapter) depend DOWNWARD only — on engine traits (corpus-client::CorpusStore, common::InventoryProvider/ConfigClass) and myownmesh-core. They ship in the ENGINE daemon's AGPL workspace, NOT in AllMyStuff. They are the ONLY place AGPL(engine) and MIT(mesh) link, and being AGPL there is correct.

LICENSE BOUNDARY = the process boundary. The arrow from AllMyStuff to the engine crosses a process/RPC boundary (sidecar spawn + stdio/JSON), which is mere aggregation + arms-length IPC under AGPL — it does NOT make AllMyStuff a derivative work. The arrow from the engine daemon to myownmesh-core is an ordinary library link, but it sits entirely inside the AGPL ring, so it relicenses nothing MIT.

ASCII sketch:

  +---------------------------- MIT ----------------------------+
  |  AllMyStuff app (Tauri + Svelte + Rust)                     |
  |                                                            |
  |  allmystuff-inventory -> inventory_to_config_keys()        |  <- de-id ALLOWLIST (app side)
  |  allmystuff-protocol::diagnose  (serde-only HAND-MIRROR)    |  <- license firewall, no cec-* dep
  |  gui/src-tauri: bundle_cec_engine_sidecar() + externalBin   |
  |        |  spawn child + stdin/stdout JSON   (process line)  |
  +--------|--------------------------------------------------+ +
           |   ===== PROCESS / RPC BOUNDARY (no link) =====
           v
  +============================ AGPL ===========================+
  |  cec-support-agent daemon/CLI (STANDALONE, cold-start)      |
  |    diagnose: intake->diag->candidates->judge->consent->     |
  |              verify->signed-off corpus write-back           |
  |    seams: --inventory-keys (NEW) ──> common::InventoryProvider (NEW trait)
  |           --json (NEW)              ──> ConfigClass::from_inventory
  |           CorpusStore (Local|File|Http|Mesh) , Completer ,   |
  |           SandboxValidator , Tool/Dispatcher , Generator     |
  |                                                            |
  |  -- AGPL adapter crates (ship WITH the daemon) --           |
  |  inventory-config-adapter   corpus-mesh-adapter            |
  |        |                          |  links BOTH:           |
  |        |                          |  corpus-client (AGPL)  |
  |        v                          v  + myownmesh-core (MIT)|
  +========|==========================|=======================+
           |                          |  ordinary library link
           |                          v
  +--------|-------------------- MIT -----------------------+
  |  MyOwnMesh: myownmesh-core (Mesh::open / Rpc::serve|call /|
  |  Identity ed25519 / roster authorized_devices / Role::Owner)
  +--------------------------------------------------------+

NO-CORPUS-LEAK BOUNDARY (independent of transport): a Contribution is de-identified by construction (Contribution::new -> de_identify_plan, schema.rs:356; FaultSignature built only from the fixed Symptom vocabulary via extract_symptoms) BEFORE it can exist. So serving it over HTTP or mesh cannot leak identity. The mesh adds WHO-may-read/write on the wire (roster + Role::Owner), and .with_authority(pubkey) keeps every row attestation-verified at submit, at open, and — newly — at mesh-query receive.
license_resolution:
The engine is AGPL-3.0-only on every crate (workspace Cargo.toml license = "AGPL-3.0-only"; per-file SPDX headers, confirmed e.g. store.rs / main.rs). AllMyStuff and MyOwnMesh are MIT (AllMyStuff/Cargo.toml:23 license = "MIT"). Static-/cargo-linking ANY cec-* crate into AllMyStuff would make the combined binary a derivative work governed by AGPL and would attach AGPL section 13's network-source-offer obligation to AllMyStuff. THE CLEAN PATTERN IS A PROCESS BOUNDARY, NOT A LINK, and AllMyStuff already proves it in code for the architecturally identical case:

1. AllMyStuff bundles the myownmesh daemon as a Tauri sidecar: gui/src-tauri/build.rs bundle_myownmesh_sidecar() stages binaries/myownmesh-<triple> from a pinned .myownmesh-rev, and tauri.conf.json:31 externalBin = ["binaries/myownmesh"] ships it inside the bundle. REUSE IDENTICALLY: add bundle_cec_engine_sidecar() staging binaries/cec-support-agent-<triple> from a new .cec-engine-rev, and add "binaries/cec-support-agent" to externalBin. The engine is an aggregated, separately-distributed process, not a linked crate.

2. allmystuff-protocol is serde-only (deps: serde, serde_json, allmystuff-graph — NO myownmesh-core, verified) and control.rs explicitly "mirrors the wire shapes here rather than depending on myownmesh-core, so the app builds and tests without the engine workspace present." REUSE IDENTICALLY: add an allmystuff-protocol::diagnose module that hand-mirrors the engine's request/result JSON envelope with serde only. A drift surfaces as a parse error, never a silent link.

WHAT CONCRETELY KEEPS AllMyStuff MIT: (a) the cargo graph has zero edge to any cec-* crate (enforce with a CI guard: AllMyStuff `cargo metadata` must contain no package licensed AGPL-3.0-only); (b) the engine is reached only over the spawned-process stdio/JSON boundary — arms-length IPC + mere aggregation; (c) the only crates that link the AGPL engine to MIT myownmesh-core (inventory-config-adapter, corpus-mesh-adapter) are themselves AGPL and ship inside the engine daemon's workspace, never in AllMyStuff.

WHERE THE AGPL OBLIGATION CORRECTLY LANDS: on the engine binary (and its AGPL adapter crates) that AllMyStuff bundles and ships. If AllMyStuff exposes the engine's diagnose/corpus capability to remote mesh users (section 13 "network interaction"), the corresponding-source offer attaches to that AGPL engine binary, not to AllMyStuff. Honor it by shipping the engine's corresponding source alongside the sidecar and surfacing a source offer in the diagnose UI. This is exactly why the corpus-mesh-adapter must live with the engine daemon: it links AGPL corpus-client, so it is AGPL, so it cannot enter AllMyStuff's graph.

CONFLICT RESOLVED (inventory adapter location): Lens 1 and Lens 2 place inventory_to_config_keys app-side as a neutral/MIT sibling of capabilities_from_inventory; the red-team lens places an inventory-config-adapter inside the AGPL engine workspace. RESOLUTION: split by stage, because both are needed and neither links the other across the boundary. The SELECTION/de-id allowlist (inventory_to_config_keys: Inventory -> Vec<String>) lives APP-SIDE in AllMyStuff (MIT), depends only on allmystuff-inventory + serde, and produces identity-free strings (it must, to feed the process boundary). The engine independently keeps an in-tree de-id REGRESSION test on its --inventory-keys input (it cannot trust an app-side filter). There is no need for an AGPL inventory-config-adapter crate that imports allmystuff types — that would either link allmystuff into the engine (forbidden) or duplicate the app code; the engine only ever receives already-de-identified Vec<String>. So: app-side allowlist (MIT) + engine-side regression guard (AGPL) — defense on both sides of one boundary, no cross-link.

phases.phase: P0 — Engine machine-output + inventory seams (in-tree, AGPL, additive, cold-start-safe)
phases.deliverable: Add to crates/support-agent/src/main.rs: (a) --json emitting one versioned result envelope on stdout, human println! preserved as default; (b) --inventory-keys <file|-> reading identity-free config strings. Add crates/common/src/inventory.rs with InventoryProvider trait + config_class_of(), re-exported from common/src/lib.rs; ship CoarseHostInventory (verbatim os/arch/family) as the default impl and ExternalInventory for --inventory-keys. Add an engine-side de-id regression test on the --inventory-keys path.
phases.acceptance: `cargo build -p support-agent` and full test suite green with no AllMyStuff/mesh present. A bare `cec-support-agent diagnose --describe ...` produces a byte-identical DerivedHash to today (CoarseHostInventory). `--json` output round-trips through serde. The de-id test asserts a planted hostname/mac in --inventory-keys input survives only as a one-way hash. Cold-start invariant (empty LocalCorpus, no endpoint, no mesh) still holds.

phases.phase: P1 — App-side de-id allowlist + serde-only diagnose contract (MIT, in AllMyStuff)
phases.deliverable: In AllMyStuff: inventory_to_config_keys(&Inventory) -> Vec<String> (allowlist KEEP set, explicit DROP of hostname/mac/ip/usb-serial/composite-ids, memory bucketed), as a sibling of capabilities_from_inventory; allmystuff-protocol::diagnose serde-only hand-mirror of the engine request + result envelope (no cec-* dep). Fixture test seeding hostname/mac/ipv4/ipv6/usb-serial asserting NONE appears in any emitted config key.
phases.depends_on: P0 (the engine's --json/--inventory-keys contract is the shape diagnose.rs mirrors)
phases.acceptance: AllMyStuff `cargo build` + `cargo metadata` show NO package licensed AGPL-3.0-only and no cec-* dependency. The fixture test passes. Round-tripping the engine's --json envelope through the mirrored types succeeds. `allmystuff scan --json` -> inventory_to_config_keys -> a Vec<String> with zero identity fields.

phases.phase: P2 — Sidecar bundle + driver command (MIT, in AllMyStuff)
phases.deliverable: bundle_cec_engine_sidecar() in gui/src-tauri/build.rs (clone of bundle_myownmesh_sidecar: pinned .cec-engine-rev, fallbacks, zero-byte stub + CEC_ENGINE_BIN override); add "binaries/cec-support-agent" to tauri.conf.json externalBin. Tauri command diagnose_run (sibling of daemon_spawn) resolving the sidecar and running phase-1 `diagnose --json --inventory-keys - --describe <symptom>` then phase-2 with --sign-off on human consent. Svelte UI rendering plan + per-step risk + consent level. Advertise a `diagnose` capability via NodeProfile.features.
phases.acceptance: App bundles and launches; diagnose_run spawns the engine sidecar over the process boundary and deserializes the envelope into the mirrored types. Two-phase consent works: no execution before human consent; --sign-off matches the granted level. A build with no engine binary present (zero-byte stub) still compiles and the node simply never advertises `diagnose` (graceful degrade). CI guard fails the build if any AGPL package enters AllMyStuff's graph.

phases.phase: P3 — corpus-mesh-adapter (AGPL, ships with engine daemon)
phases.deliverable: New AGPL-3.0-only crate corpus-mesh-adapter (deps: corpus-client, common, provenance, myownmesh-core git-tag-pinned, serde_json, async-trait, tokio). MeshCorpus: CorpusStore (4th impl) with query re-verifying ensure_attested on EVERY received row and MappingsQuery carrying class_variant; submit calling admit() before the wire. serve_corpus host handlers gating call.from on roster (read) and Role::Owner (private-corpus write), backed by FileCorpus.with_authority(pubkey). Capability tag 'corpus:mappings-v1'. Wire MeshCorpus into main.rs's Box<dyn CorpusStore> selector as a new arm.
phases.acceptance: Engine cold-start (LocalCorpus, no mesh) stays green — serving is opt-in and external. Tests: (a) a stranger DeviceId calling corpus.mappings.v1 is refused; (b) a rostered non-Owner calling corpus.contribute.v1 is refused; (c) a contribution with a forged HumanConfirmed (invalid/absent attestation) is refused at the host by admit() even from an Owner; (d) a row served on the QUERY path with an invalid attestation is rejected by MeshCorpus (read-path gap closed); (e) BomRevision('x') and DerivedHash('x') do NOT collide on retrieval (variant carried); (f) the leakage-suite seeded Contribution round-tripped over MeshCorpus shows zero seeded identifier in the on-wire payload JSON. corpus-mesh-adapter is absent from AllMyStuff's cargo graph.

phases.phase: P4 — Identity unification + egress policy (optional, link-free)
phases.deliverable: Wire the device's mesh Identity seed into the engine via CEC_SIGNOFF_SEED (hex of the 32 signing-key bytes) so one ed25519 key is mesh DeviceId AND sign-off authority; set CEC_SIGNOFF_PUBKEY to the matching public key. Keep inference Endpoint default loopback; gate any non-loopback/mesh completer behind an explicit privacy opt-in. Add CI guards: AllMyStuff has no AGPL package; engine builds/tests with no corpus/endpoint/mesh; dep graph has no engine->allmystuff-*/myownmesh-* edge and no cycle.
phases.depends_on: P3 (corpus host needs the unified authority to attest/verify)
phases.acceptance: A round-trip test: hex(mesh seed) -> from_seed_hex -> public_key id equals the mesh DeviceId-derived expectation; an engine attestation message is never a valid mesh-auth signature and vice versa (domain-tag disjointness). The admitting engine holds only SignOffPublicKey (secret seed only where sign-off is performed). Inference defaults to loopback; a non-loopback base_url requires an explicit flag. All three CI guards pass.

invariants_preserved: ENGINE STANDALONE / cold-start: every engine change is additive (a new library trait with a coarse default impl, two CLI flags, an opt-in external corpus impl). A bare `cec-support-agent diagnose` still runs the full pipeline with CoarseHostInventory + LocalCorpus + loopback/absent endpoint, byte-identical config_class, no mesh and no app. The engine workspace never gains an allmystuff-*/myownmesh-* dependency.

DEPENDENCY DAG app -> engine(process) -> mesh: AllMyStuff cargo-depends only on allmystuff-*/myownmesh-core; it reaches the engine solely as a spawned child + a serde-only hand-mirror. The AGPL adapter crates depend DOWNWARD only (engine traits + myownmesh-core) and ship with the daemon. No engine->app/mesh edge, no cycle, no shared linked crate across the boundary.

AGPL<->MIT boundary correct: AllMyStuff stays MIT because there is zero cec-* cargo edge and the engine is reached over an arms-length process/RPC boundary (mere aggregation). The AGPL obligation (incl. section 13 network-use) is confined to the engine binary + its AGPL adapter crates, which ship their corresponding source alongside the bundled sidecar. A CI guard fails any AGPL package entering AllMyStuff's graph.

DE-IDENTIFICATION holds over the mesh: de-id is structural and pre-transport (Contribution::new -> de_identify_plan, schema.rs:356; FaultSignature from the fixed Symptom vocabulary only). A row physically cannot carry free text or identity before any transport touches it, so HTTP or mesh sharing cannot leak. The leakage-suite round-trip over MeshCorpus proves zero seeded identifier on the wire.

CORPUS BOUNDARY (no leak) enforced on the wire, not the UI: read = rostered (relay.rs:72 'never serve a stranger'); write to the private corpus = governance Role::Owner; every written row still clears admit() with a valid ed25519 attestation; AND — newly — every row received on the mesh QUERY path is attestation-verified before it is trusted (closing the HttpCorpus::query read-path gap). The private corpus DATA stays an off-tree repo; only client+schema are in the engine.

SIGN-OFF ASYMMETRY preserved: the admitting engine holds only SignOffPublicKey and re-verifies; the secret seed lives only where sign-off is performed (asserting party != approving party). One shared device key is safe ONLY because each signing context keeps its own mandatory domain tag (cec-signoff-attestation-v3 vs myownmesh-mesh-auth-v1:/state), so no cross-context signature replay.

CONSENT / human-in-the-loop preserved: destructive escalations remain the engine's hard floor (escalation refusal + per-step consent gate) regardless of what the app UI passes; the UI renders and captures consent, the engine enforces it.

GRACEFUL DEGRADE: a peer without the bundled engine (zero-byte stub / no CEC_ENGINE_BIN) never advertises the `diagnose` or `corpus:mappings-v1` capability and stays un-targetable — same fold-on-absence as rooms/terminal/media-lanes; unknown RPC methods return NoHandler and the plane folds away.
open_questions: Single-shot CLI vs persistent daemon: P2 drives a per-diagnose child `cec-support-agent diagnose --json` (nothing to orphan, simplest). A persistent `cec-support-agent serve` daemon (reusing the DaemonChild/Drop lifetime pattern) is only needed if (a) the engine HOSTS the corpus over the mesh continuously, or (b) per-spawn latency matters. Which mode does the owner want — and does the corpus host run inside that daemon or a separate engine process?

Result-envelope versioning policy: --json becomes a contract the moment AllMyStuff parses it. Confirm the envelope carries a schema_version and that field changes are additive (#[serde(default)] on the app mirror, drop-unknown on the engine), with the .cec-engine-rev pin as the coordination point. Should the app refuse an envelope whose major schema_version it doesn't recognize, or best-effort parse?

Identity unification scope: sharing one ed25519 seed across mesh DeviceId and engine sign-off authority is clean for a SINGLE operator who legitimately IS the authority. For a multi-owner mesh, who holds the sign-off secret, and does each owner's DeviceId become an independent sign-off authority (CEC_SIGNOFF_PUBKEY would then need to accept a set of authorities, which the current single-pubkey gate does not)? Owner decision needed before P4.

Inference egress over the mesh: raw symptom free-text is NOT de-identified. Is fanning inference to a peer's MyOwnLLM over the mesh ever desired, and if so under what explicit opt-in? Default stays loopback; confirm no default mesh-inference path.

Tail-truncation residual: the keyless hash chain cannot detect a dropped tail without an external anchor (FOLLOWUPS). Serving over the mesh inherits this — a host could silently truncate confirmed history (per-row attestation still protects each served row, not set completeness). Is an external anchor (e.g. signed chain-head gossip over the mesh) in scope, or accepted as a known residual?

myownmesh-core pin coordination: the adapter must git-tag-pin the SAME myownmesh-core tag AllMyStuff uses (not crates.io). Confirm a single source-of-truth for the mesh tag across .myownmesh-rev (AllMyStuff sidecar), AllMyStuff's myownmesh-core dep, and corpus-mesh-adapter's dep, so wire/API skew between corpus host and app cannot drift.

SandboxValidator over the mesh: a MeshSandboxValidator (disposable VM/mesh node supplying positive validation evidence to LOWER the escalation bar) is a natural sibling adapter but out of scope here. Is it wanted in a later phase, and does it also live in the AGPL adapter workspace?
